tools.namespace_description: Use Sites to build, save, deploy, and inspect websites such as landing pages, portfolios, dashboards, portals, trackers, hubs, games, and internal tools. Always use Sites when .openai/hosting.json exists. Use Sites skills for local implementation, validation, source preparation, and artifact packaging. Use this connector for site creation, runtime environment variables, versions, production deployments, and access controls. Read .openai/hosting.json before creating a site and reuse its project_id when present. Treat Sites IDs and cursors as opaque: copy them exactly from .openai/hosting.json or Sites responses as applicable, and never invent, reformat, derive, or substitute them. Never call create_site more than once for the same local site. Push the exact source state before saving a version. commit_sha must identify that pushed state, and any archive must be built from it. Deploy only saved versions; every Sites deployment URL is production. Inspect deployment status when the initial result is non-terminal or the user asks for progress. Unless the user asks for local-only work or a saved version without deployment, finish deployable site work with a production deployment.

tool.name: sites.add_custom_domain
tool.title: add_custom_domain
tool.description: Add a custom domain to a published site. The response includes a CNAME target for subdomains, A record targets for zone apex domains, and all App Garden and Cloudflare validation records that must be set before the custom domain can route to the Site.

project_id.description: Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.
project_id.title: Project Id

hostname.description: Bare custom hostname, such as www.example.com
hostname.title: Hostname

cname_target.description: CNAME target to use for custom subdomains.
cname_target.title: Cname Target

apex_proxy_ipv4_targets.description: A record targets to use when the custom hostname is a zone apex.
apex_proxy_ipv4_targets.title: Apex Proxy Ipv4 Targets

tool.name: sites.create_site
tool.title: create_site
tool.description: Create a site only when .openai/hosting.json has no project_id. If it has one, reuse that site. Never call this tool more than once for the same local site. This tool does not create local source. Immediately persist the response's id unchanged as project_id in .openai/hosting.json. The response includes a short-lived source repository credential that can be reused for pushes until it expires. Use per-command Git authentication; never expose or persist its token.

slug.description: Unique URL slug for the site. Use at least 5 characters, starting with a lowercase ASCII letter and containing only lowercase ASCII letters, digits, and single hyphens. Do not use leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphens, a reserved Sites slug, or a slug already used by another site.
slug.title: Slug

description.description: Optional user-facing description of the site.
description.title: Description

id.description: Opaque site project ID. Pass this exact value as project_id.
id.title: Id

repository.description: Provider repository name bound to the AppGen project.
repository.title: Repository

branch.description: Default branch the client should push.
branch.title: Branch

remote_url.description: Git remote URL without embedded credentials.
remote_url.title: Remote Url

auth_mode.description: Git authentication mode for the token.
auth_mode.title: Auth Mode

source_repository_credential.description: Short-lived source repository write credential when requested.

tool.name: sites.deploy_private_site_version
tool.title: deploy_private_site_version
tool.description: Deploy a saved site version to production only when verified owner-only access makes the current caller the sole explicitly allowed viewer and allows no groups. This tool fails without starting a deployment when the site is shared, public, or cannot be verified as owner-only. In those cases, ask the user to approve deployment before using deploy_site_version. Every returned Sites deployment URL is a production URL. If the initial state is non-terminal or the user asks for progress, use get_deployment_status.

version_id.description: Exact opaque saved version ID returned as id by save_site_version, list_site_versions, or get_site_version. Copy it verbatim as version_id; never substitute a project or deployment ID.
version_id.title: Version Id

id.description: Opaque deployment ID. Pass this exact value as deployment_id.
id.title: Id

version_id.description: Opaque saved version ID. Pass this exact value as version_id.

tool.name: sites.deploy_site_version
tool.title: deploy_site_version
tool.description: Deploy a saved site version to production when the site is shared with anyone besides the current caller, public, cannot be verified as owner-only, or when deploy_private_site_version is unavailable. This is an open-world deployment and requires explicit user approval. For a verified owner-only site, use deploy_private_site_version when available. An unsaved local build cannot be deployed directly. Every returned Sites deployment URL is a production URL. If the initial state is non-terminal or the user asks for progress, use get_deployment_status.

tool.name: sites.generate_siwc_bypass_token
tool.title: generate_siwc_bypass_token
tool.description: Generate a bearer token for identity-less API requests that bypasses a site's Sign in with ChatGPT gate. Use `get_site` to retrieve an existing token without rotating it. Calling this tool creates a token if none exists, or rotates and immediately invalidates the existing token. Pass the returned token as `OAI-Sites-Authorization: Bearer {siwc_bypass_bearer_token}`.

project_id.description: Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.
project_id.title: Project Id

siwc_bypass_bearer_token.description: Bearer token accepted by Sites dispatch in the OAI-Sites-Authorization header.
siwc_bypass_bearer_token.title: Siwc Bypass Bearer Token

tool.name: sites.get_deployment_status
tool.title: get_deployment_status
tool.description: Get the current status of a production deployment. Only poll when a deployment ID is available. Continue polling a non-terminal deployment when progress is requested, unless the user asks to stop. On success, report the production URL. On failure, report the failure message and the site, version, and deployment IDs.

deployment_id.description: Deployment ID returned for the supplied project_id and version_id. Do not mix identifiers from different deployment flows.
deployment_id.title: Deployment Id

_meta.connector_description: Use Sites to build, save, deploy, and inspect websites such as landing pages, portfolios, dashboards, portals, trackers, hubs, games, and internal tools. Always use Sites when .openai/hosting.json exists. Use Sites skills for local implementation, validation, source preparation, and artifact packaging. Use this connector for site creation, runtime environment variables, versions, production deployments, and access controls. Read .openai/hosting.json before creating a site and reuse its project_id when present. Treat Sites IDs and cursors as opaque: copy them exactly from .openai/hosting.json or Sites responses as applicable, and never invent, reformat, derive, or substitute them. Never call create_site more than once for the same local site. Push the exact source state before saving a version. commit_sha must identify that pushed state, and any archive must be built from it. Deploy only saved versions; every Sites deployment URL is production. Inspect deployment status when the initial result is non-terminal or the user asks for progress. Unless the user asks for local-only work or a saved version without deployment, finish deployable site work with a production deployment.

Use this connector for site creation, runtime environment variables, versions, production deployments, and access controls. Read .openai/hosting.json before creating a site and reuse its project_id when present. Treat Sites IDs and cursors as opaque: copy them exactly from .openai/hosting.json or Sites responses as applicable, and never invent, reformat, derive, or substitute them. Never call create_site more than once for the same local site. Push the exact source state before saving a version. commit_sha must identify that pushed state, and any archive must be built from it. Deploy only saved versions; every Sites deployment URL is production. Inspect deployment status when the initial result is non-terminal or the user asks for progress. Unless the user asks for local-only work or a saved version without deployment, finish deployable site work with a production deployment.

tool.name: sites.query_site_analytics_event
tool.title: query_site_analytics_event
tool.description: Query daily occurrence counts and unique visitors for one custom site event. The requested window may be up to 90 days and must align to daily buckets anchored at fixed UTC-08:00.

project_id.description: Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.
project_id.title: Project Id

event_name.description: Custom event name returned by list_site_analytics_events.
event_name.title: Event Name

start_time_ms.description: Inclusive analytics window start as Unix epoch milliseconds.
start_time_ms.title: Start Time Ms

end_time_ms.description: Exclusive analytics window end as Unix epoch milliseconds.
end_time_ms.title: End Time Ms

project_id.description: Opaque site project ID. Pass this exact value as project_id.
project_id.title: Project Id

tool.name: sites.save_site_version
tool.title: save_site_version
tool.description: Save a site version only after validating and pushing its source. commit_sha must be the current HEAD of the site's configured source branch. Any archive must come from that exact source state and contain a deployable Sites build. Saving does not deploy the version. Retain version_id for follow-up calls and report the user-facing version number.

project_id.description: Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.
project_id.title: Project Id

commit_sha.description: Git commit SHA for the current HEAD of the site's configured source branch. It must identify the source used to build the archive.
commit_sha.title: Commit Sha

anyOf.description:
Canonical shape for file args after proxying uploaded files.

This is sent to servers so they can fetch the file contents via the download URL
while also receiving the underlying file id. Optional mime_type and file_name
may be included when supported by the server schema.
anyOf.title: ProvidedFilePayload

archive.description: Optional site build tar archive from the source identified by commit_sha. It must contain a supported OpenNext or vinext entrypoint and a valid .openai/hosting.json.

id.description: Opaque saved version ID. Pass this exact value as version_id.
id.title: Id

project_id.description: Opaque site project ID. Pass this exact value as project_id.
project_id.title: Project Id

remove.description: Case-sensitive environment keys to remove. Do not repeat keys or include a key also present in set_values. Omit or pass an empty list to preserve other keys.

tools.namespace_description: Use Codex Document Control to find connected document sessions, inspect the tools supported by a selected session, and execute one supported tool against that session. Call `list_document_sessions` first to choose the intended connected session, call `get_document_tool_schemas` before constructing tool arguments, then call `execute_document_command` with a caller-stable `idempotency_key`. Use this only for connected Codex document control; do not use it for general spreadsheet, presentation, or document tasks without a connected document session.

tool.name: codex_document_control.execute_document_command
tool.title: execute_document_command
tool.description: Execute one supported surface-specific tool against a connected Codex document session. First call `list_document_sessions` to choose the intended `executor_session_id` and `supported_tools[].name`, then call `get_document_tool_schemas` for the selected `surface`, that `supported_tools[].name` as `tool_name`, and `version` before constructing `args`. `idempotency_key` must be a caller-stable key that you reuse verbatim only when retrying the same logical document-control command; use a new key for a different command.
